name: xss-html-injection
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to \"test for XSS vulnerabilities\", \"perform cross-site scripting attacks\", \"identify HTML injection flaws\", \"exploit client-side injection...
capabilities: Identify stored, reflected, and DOM-based XSS vulnerability locations
Test input fields for HTML injection and script injection points
Exploit XSS to demonstrate session hijacking and credential theft
Bypass common XSS filters using encoding and obfuscation techniques
Validate input sanitization and output encoding effectiveness
Generate proof-of-concept payloads for vulnerability reports
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate security testing skill for XSS and HTML injection vulnerability assessment. All 121 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - they represent educational examples of vulnerable code patterns (eval, innerHTML, document.write) and demonstration payloads using example URLs (attacker.com). The skill teaches security professionals how to identify and test for client-side injection vulnerabilities in authorized penetration tests.
valueStatement: Web applications often contain cross-site scripting and HTML injection flaws that attackers exploit to steal sessions and credentials. This skill provides systematic methodology for detecting, exploiting, and validating client-side injection vulnerabilities in authorized security assessments.
userTitle: Test for XSS and HTML injection vulnerabilities
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization before testing
Cannot test applications behind authentication without test accounts
Content Security Policy may block inline script testing
HttpOnly cookies prevent JavaScript-based session theft

useCases.title: Security Consultant Testing Web Applications
useCases.description: Conduct authorized penetration tests to identify XSS vulnerabilities in client web applications and provide remediation recommendations.

useCases.title: Developer Validating Input Handling
useCases.description: Test custom web applications during development to verify proper input sanitization and output encoding are implemented.

useCases.title: QA Engineer Performing Security Regression
useCases.description: Include XSS and HTML injection testing in security regression test suites to catch vulnerabilities before production deployment.

promptTemplates.title: Basic XSS Detection
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the XSS HTML injection skill to test the login form at https://example.com for reflected XSS vulnerabilities. Identify all input fields and test for basic XSS payloads.
promptTemplates.scenario: Testing a specific page for reflected XSS

promptTemplates.title: Stored XSS Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the XSS HTML injection skill to test the comment section for stored XSS. Create test payloads that persist and execute when other users view the content.
promptTemplates.scenario: Testing stored XSS in user-generated content

promptTemplates.title: DOM-Based XSS Discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the XSS HTML injection skill to analyze the JavaScript on https://example.com/dashboard for DOM-based XSS. Check location.hash and location.search handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding client-side XSS in JavaScript code

promptTemplates.title: Filter Bypass Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the XSS HTML injection skill to test if the WAF at https://example.com blocks common XSS payloads. Try HTML encoding, Unicode encoding, and tag variation bypass techniques.
promptTemplates.scenario: Testing WAF effectiveness against encoded payloads

bestPractices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any target application
Use controlled demonstration payloads that do not persist or spread to unintended users
Report critical vulnerabilities immediately through proper incident response channels
antiPatterns: Testing production systems without explicit written authorization
Using discovered vulnerabilities for unauthorized access or data exfiltration
Deploying payloads that could cause denial of service or system damage

faq.question: Does this skill perform actual attacks on target websites?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides methodology and payloads for authorized security testing. Users must have explicit written permission before testing any system.

faq.question: Can this skill test for all types of XSS?
faq.answer: The skill covers stored, reflected, and DOM-based XSS. However, some advanced variants like mutation XSS may require additional specialized techniques.

faq.question: What authorization is required before using this skill?
faq.answer: Written authorization from the system owner is required. This should include defined scope, permitted testing methods, and handling procedures for any captured data.

faq.question: Does this skill work against applications with CSP?
faq.answer: CSP can block many XSS payloads. The skill includes techniques for CSP bypass testing, but some well-configured CSP policies may prevent successful exploitation.

faq.question: Can session cookies always be stolen via XSS?
faq.answer: No. Cookies marked with HttpOnly cannot be accessed via JavaScript. Modern applications should use this protection for session cookies.

faq.question: What should I do if I find a critical XSS vulnerability?
faq.answer: Report immediately through proper channels per your authorization agreement. Document the finding with proof-of-concept and provide remediation recommendations to the owner.
